name: claude-code
description: Claude Code project with CLAUDE.md + MemKraft memory structure + 3 example entries

files.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file guides Claude Code in this project.

## Memory System

This project uses **MemKraft** (https://pypi.org/project/memkraft/) for compound memory.

### Core rules
1. Before answering questions about prior work, people, or decisions: run `memkraft search "<query>"` first.
2. When the user shares durable information (a person, an org, a decision), store it:
   - `memkraft track <name> --type person|company|concept`
   - `memkraft update <name> --info "..."`
3. For time-sensitive facts, use `memkraft fact-add` via the Python API.
4. At the end of a session, run `memkraft dream` for memory maintenance (can be cronned).

### Structure
- `memory/entities/` — people, orgs, projects
- `memory/decisions/` — why we did what we did
- `memory/inbox/` — raw capture, processed later
- `memory/live-notes/` — ongoing tracking pages

### Doctor
Run `memkraft doctor` periodically to check memory health.
Run `memkraft doctor --fix` to auto-repair missing directories.

# Decision: Use MemKraft for compound memory

## Context
We needed a zero-dependency, file-native memory layer for AI agents.

## Decision
Adopt MemKraft as the durable memory substrate.

## Why
- Markdown-first (human-readable + diffable)
- Zero DB requirements for the core features
- Works with Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI, MCP

## Alternatives considered
- Pure vector DB (too heavy, no human-readable surface)
- Plain text notes (no structure, no search)
